message:
perf: protocol, raptorq, kernel, cbor, and core small optimizations and cleanup

fcp-protocol:
- Add #[inline] to validate_frame_lengths() and mac_input() — both are
  called per-frame in the hot path and benefit from inlining.
- Replace intermediate byte-array copy in FcpsDatagram::from_bytes()
  seq parsing with direct u64::from_le_bytes() from individual bytes,
  avoiding a temporary [u8; 8] allocation.
- Add comment clarifying bounds guarantee for direct byte access.

fcp-raptorq:
- Switch rhs[row] = data.clone() to rhs[row].clone_from(&data) in the
  dense reconstruction path, allowing reuse of existing allocations.
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] on try_reconstruct_dense() — this is an
  intentional fallback path kept for correctness reference.

fcp-kernel:
- Rename ProvisioningStatus::AwaitingHuman to AwaitingUser in test
  assertions, aligning with the upstream enum rename.

fcp-cbor:
- Refactor SchemaId::hash() to call std::io::Write::write_fmt()
  directly instead of using the write!() macro through a use-import,
  making the io::Write trait usage explicit and grep-friendly.

fcp-core:
- Add #[allow(clippy::redundant_pub_crate)] on pattern_matches() to
  suppress false-positive lint on the pub(crate) visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
